column1: # Authenticity Contract — labels spill into adjacent empty cells.
# Classic Lotus 1-2-3 behavior: a label wider than its own column bleeds
# into the next cell(s) — but only while those cells are empty. The
#   ^  (caret)                 → spill both ways, centered in the span
#   \  (backslash / fill)      → no spill; the pattern fills own width
# A non-empty neighbor hard-stops the spill and the label truncates at
# --- Left-align spill (apostrophe, the implicit default prefix) ---
# "INCOME SUMMARY 1991" is 19 chars; default column width is 9.
# B1 and C1 are empty, so the label paints across A1..C1.
# --- Spill hard-stopped by a non-empty neighbor ---
# Put a long label in A2, then "X" in B2. B2 is occupied, so A2 must
# truncate at its own 9-char boundary — no bleed into B2.
# Type a long right-aligned label in B3. A3 is empty, so the overflow
# flows into A3. "right-align spill" is 17 chars; combined A3+B3 = 18
# → 1 pad space on the left, text right-aligned to B3's right edge.
# --- Centered spill (caret prefix) bleeds both ways ---
# "centered text" is 13 chars. Put it at C4 with B4 and D4 empty; the
# combined span is 27 chars. Centered (27-13=14 → 7 pad each side).
